records.ety: [OE. slendre, sclendre, fr. OD. slinder thin, slender, perhaps through a French form; cf. OD. slinderen, slidderen, to creep; perh. akin to E. slide.]

defs: Small or narrow in proportion to the length or the height; not thick; slim; as, a slender stem or stalk of a plant.
quotes: She, as a veil down to the slender waist, Her unadorned golden tresses wore.

defs: Weak; feeble; not strong; slight; as, slender hope; a slender constitution.
quotes: Mighty hearts are held in slender chains.
They have inferred much from slender premises.
The slender utterance of the consonants.

defs: Moderate; trivial; inconsiderable; slight; as, a man of slender intelligence.
quotes: A slender degree of patience will enable him to enjoy both the humor and the pathos.

defs: Small; inadequate; meager; pitiful; as, slender means of support; a slender pittance.
Spare; abstemious; frugal; as, a slender diet.
quotes: The good Ostorius often deigned To grace my slender table with his presence.

defs: Uttered with a thin tone; -- the opposite of broad; as, the slender vowels long e and i.
